raw:
```json
{
  "criterion": "api_design",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The API hits all the anchor-3 marks but stops short of 4. Validation passes (`openapi-validation.log:1` 'is valid'). Coverage matches the implementation: every endpoint exercised by the probe (`/books`, `/books/{id}`, `/members`, `/members/{id}`, `/loans`, `/reservations`, `/reservations/expire`) appears in `openapi.yaml`, and additional resources `/loans/{id}/return`, `/reservations/{id}/cancel`, `/fines/{id}/pay`, plus member sub-collections, are all documented. URL shape is REST-clean: plural nouns, hierarchical (`/members/{id}/fines`, `/loans/{id}/return`), no `/borrowBook`-style mixing. Status codes match the spec exactly — 201 on POST /books and POST /members, 404 on missing IDs, 400 with field-level zod `details` on bad input (api-probe.json:5,9,10), and the loan handler maps domain errors to 402/403/409 distinctly (`loan-handler.ts:28-31`). Error format is consistent across all probed responses: `{\"error\":\"...\"}` (with optional `details` on 400). Both `createLoanSchema` and `createReservationSchema` use zod (`loan-handler.ts:9-12`, `reservation-handler.ts:9-12`).\n\nIt does not reach 4 for several reasons: (1) error responses carry only a human `error` message — no stable `code` field for programmatic handling; (2) the OpenAPI `Error` schema (`openapi.yaml:583-589`) only declares `error: string` and omits the `details` array that 400 responses actually return, so the spec under-describes the implementation; (3) no examples are provided for negative ACs; (4) response envelopes are mostly predictable but cancel and expire return `{success: true}` rather than the resource. It also misses the anchor-5 bar: error→HTTP mapping is duplicated in each handler's try/catch (`loan-handler.ts:21-35`, `reservation-handler.ts:21-31`) rather than centralized in a single middleware — the file labeled `error-middleware.ts` is actually just the error class definitions, with no Express middleware. Schemas are reasonably tight (no `additionalProperties: true`, explicit `nullable: true` only where appropriate)."
}
```

parsed.rationale:
The API hits all the anchor-3 marks but stops short of 4. Validation passes (`openapi-validation.log:1` 'is valid'). Coverage matches the implementation: every endpoint exercised by the probe (`/books`, `/books/{id}`, `/members`, `/members/{id}`, `/loans`, `/reservations`, `/reservations/expire`) appears in `openapi.yaml`, and additional resources `/loans/{id}/return`, `/reservations/{id}/cancel`, `/fines/{id}/pay`, plus member sub-collections, are all documented. URL shape is REST-clean: plural nouns, hierarchical (`/members/{id}/fines`, `/loans/{id}/return`), no `/borrowBook`-style mixing. Status codes match the spec exactly — 201 on POST /books and POST /members, 404 on missing IDs, 400 with field-level zod `details` on bad input (api-probe.json:5,9,10), and the loan handler maps domain errors to 402/403/409 distinctly (`loan-handler.ts:28-31`). Error format is consistent across all probed responses: `{"error":"..."}` (with optional `details` on 400). Both `createLoanSchema` and `createReservationSchema` use zod (`loan-handler.ts:9-12`, `reservation-handler.ts:9-12`).

It does not reach 4 for several reasons: (1) error responses carry only a human `error` message — no stable `code` field for programmatic handling; (2) the OpenAPI `Error` schema (`openapi.yaml:583-589`) only declares `error: string` and omits the `details` array that 400 responses actually return, so the spec under-describes the implementation; (3) no examples are provided for negative ACs; (4) response envelopes are mostly predictable but cancel and expire return `{success: true}` rather than the resource. It also misses the anchor-5 bar: error→HTTP mapping is duplicated in each handler's try/catch (`loan-handler.ts:21-35`, `reservation-handler.ts:21-31`) rather than centralized in a single middleware — the file labeled `error-middleware.ts` is actually just the error class definitions, with no Express middleware. Schemas are reasonably tight (no `additionalProperties: true`, explicit `nullable: true` only where appropriate).
